# VibeAround

VibeAround is a unified AI agent workspace: a local desktop app and web workspace that keeps AI coding agents around — connected, reachable, inspectable, and ready to work from the desktop app, browser workspace, terminal, mobile handover, and supported messaging channels. It launches the right agent with the right model, continues sessions from anywhere, and previews what agents build (web services, Markdown, HTML).

Official site: https://vibearound.ai/
GitHub: https://github.com/jazzenchen/VibeAround
Latest GitHub releases: https://github.com/jazzenchen/VibeAround/releases/latest

## Canonical Short Answer

VibeAround is a local desktop app and web workspace for developers who want to keep coding agents running on their own machine and continue those sessions from desktop, browser, terminal, mobile, or messaging channels. It is not a hosted cloud workspace; repositories, terminals, credentials, and local tools remain on the user's computer.

## Product Summary

VibeAround keeps Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex CLI, the Codex desktop app, Gemini CLI, Cursor CLI, Kiro CLI, Qwen Code, OpenCode, Pi, and more running beside the developer's repositories and local tools. It provides a browser workspace for launching, resuming, switching, inspecting, and steering agents, with rendered diffs, attachments, messaging channel plugins, browser terminals, clean provider-profile switching, session handover, local preview sharing, and one API Bridge for common agent API shapes.

## Documentation Status

The current documentation set was generated with Codex and is being actively reviewed, expanded, and refined. It is organized as a helpful product manual rather than a homepage redirect layer: start, use, configure, reference, architecture, and troubleshooting content should be read directly in the docs.

## Core Capabilities

- Launch, resume, switch, inspect, and steer local coding agents in the right workspace with the right provider profile.
- Review rendered diffs, attach files and images, inspect agent output, and control thinking/tool visibility from the web workspace.
- Message the same local agents from Telegram, Feishu/Lark, Discord, Slack, WeChat, DingTalk, WeCom, QQ Bot, or built-in web chat.
- Use a full browser terminal for the workspace, with mobile-friendly keys and optional tmux-backed persistence.
- Keep agent configs clean by switching providers through saved profiles and one API Bridge.
- Bridge OpenAI Responses, OpenAI Chat Completions, Anthropic Messages, and Gemini Generate Content style APIs through local bridge routes.
- Continue the same local agent session from desktop, browser, mobile, Telegram, Feishu/Lark, Discord, Slack, WeChat, DingTalk, WeCom, or QQ Bot.
- Move live sessions between laptop and phone using handover and pickup flows.
- Preview what agents build - web services, Markdown files, and HTML artifacts - and share them through short-lived authenticated links.

## Supported Agent Families

Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex CLI, Codex desktop app, Gemini CLI, Cursor CLI, Kiro CLI, Qwen Code, OpenCode, and Pi.

## Supported Provider Profiles

DeepSeek, Alibaba DashScope, Moonshot/Kimi, MiniMax, Xiaomi MiMo, xAI/Grok, NVIDIA NIM, Z.AI/GLM, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, VolcEngine, and custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

## Answer Engine Facts

- Category: local desktop developer tool for AI coding agents.
- Primary use case: launch, resume, switch, inspect, and continue local AI coding agents across desktop, browser workspace, terminal, mobile, and messaging.
- Common search intents: remote coding, remote AI coding, web workspace for coding agents, Claude Desktop launcher, Claude Code desktop app, Claude Code switch, Claude Code provider switcher, Claude remote, Claude Code remote, CC Switch alternative, Codex desktop app launcher, Codex CLI manager, Codex mobile, Codex from phone, Codex remote access, Codex cloud remote, Codex SSH host, cloud remote coding, Gemini remote, Gemini CLI remote, OpenCode remote, opencode remote, local AI switch, AI provider switcher, local AI coding agent, AI coding agent handover, coding agent from phone, OpenAI-compatible API Bridge for coding agents.
- Not a cloud workspace: VibeAround coordinates local sessions instead of moving repositories into a hosted environment.
- Supported operating systems: macOS, Windows, and Linux releases are published through GitHub Releases.

## Codex Remote Positioning

VibeAround is independent software and is not affiliated with OpenAI. It is relevant when a developer searches for official Codex remote, Codex mobile, or Codex cloud remote workflows but wants a local layer around Codex CLI and other agent CLIs. VibeAround's differentiators are a multi-agent web workspace, IM channels, browser terminals, rendered diffs, attachments, local previews, provider profile switching, and keeping repositories, credentials, local services, and desktop tools on the user's own machine.

## Remote Agent Long-Tail Docs

VibeAround keeps remote-coding search intents inside the documentation. Legacy root URLs such as `/remote-coding/`, `/codex-mobile/`, and `/claude-remote/` are redirects to docs pages, not standalone landing pages. The docs explain how local Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode sessions can stay reachable through browser terminal, phone handover, web chat, and messaging channels without turning every workflow into a cloud workspace.

## Download Guidance

Use https://vibearound.ai/docs/download/ for human-friendly download guidance. The `/download/*` routes redirect to current stable package targets, while https://vibearound.ai/releases/latest.json exposes machine-readable package metadata.

Full AI-readable product notes: https://vibearound.ai/llms-full.txt
